sections.paragraph:
pl. תַּנִּינִים m. Arab. تنّيِنُ (from the root תָּנַן No. 1).-

(1) a sea monster, a vast fish, Gr. κῆτος, Gen 1:21 Job 7:12 Isa 27:1.

(2) a serpent, Exo 7:9, seqq.; Deu 32:33 Psa 91:13 a dragon, Jer 51:34 a crocodile, Eze 29:3 (where there is תַּנֹּים, which see, for תַּנִּין), which is used as an image of Egypt, Isa 51:9 Eze. loc. cit., and 32:2 ( Psa 74:13, 14 Psa 74:14). Compare תָּן.
